entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation Call from SAP UI5 Application throws error
entry.content:
Hi, I'm getting 401 unauthorized error while calling Build Workflow API trigger from my SAPUI5 application (both locally running or from Build workzone). The URL called is "https://spa-api-gateway-bpi-us-prod.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instance..." to get X-CSRF token. My Workflow created as per tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-academy-run-salesorderprocess.html#fb19e3da-bee0-4477-afc4-... Can you suggest what is the possible resolution for this issue.

What is the correct URL to trigger the BPA workflow API from the SAP UI5 app. And what is the correct method to call the API ?

1. Calling SBPA Workflow API to trigger process from SAPUI5 app

2. 401 unauthorized error -

3. CORS issue while testing UI5 app locally or from Build Workzone

Hi All

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Part 1: SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR): Foundation for Modern Revenue Management
entry.content:
In today's complex business environment, managing revenue recognition has become increasingly challenging, especially with the introduction of standards like IFRS 15 and ASC 606. Organizations across industries are grappling with intricate revenue streams, diverse contract types, and stringent compliance requirements. SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) emerges as a comprehensive solution to these challenges, providing automated and standardized revenue recognition processes that ensure compliance while improving efficiency.

The Evolution of Revenue Recognition

Revenue recognition has transformed significantly from simple invoice-based accounting to a sophisticated process that considers multiple performance obligations, complex pricing structures, and varying recognition patterns. SAP RAR addresses these modern requirements by providing a robust foundation built on three core pillars: contract management, revenue recognition engine, and seamless integration capabilities.

 

Understanding the Core Process

The journey of revenue recognition in SAP RAR represents a sophisticated orchestration of multiple components working together to ensure accurate and compliant revenue processing. Let's explore this through a comprehensive real-world scenario that demonstrates the system's capabilities.

Consider TechServe Solutions, a technology company that offers both hardware and subscription-based software services. They've recently closed a deal that includes:

  • Enterprise Server Hardware: $100,000
  • Software License: $50,000/year for 3 years
  • Implementation Services: $30,000
  • Maintenance Support: $20,000/year for 3 years

Contract Inception and RAI Creation

When the sales order is created, SAP RAR begins its sophisticated processing journey. The system automatically generates Revenue Accounting Items (RAIs) for each component of the deal. These RAIs serve as the foundation for all subsequent revenue recognition processes. During this phase, the system captures critical information including contract terms, pricing details, and delivery schedules.

Performance Obligation Breakdown

SAP RAR's intelligent contract analysis capabilities automatically identify and separate distinct performance obligations within the contract. 

Price Allocation and SSP Determination

One of the most complex aspects of revenue recognition is the fair allocation of transaction prices. SAP RAR handles this through its sophisticated Standalone Selling Price (SSP) engine. For TechServe's contract:

The total contract value of $280,000 ($100,000 + $150,000 + $30,000 + $60,000) is allocated based on relative SSP values. SAP RAR automatically calculates these allocations while considering:

  • Market-based pricing data
  • Historical transaction analysis
  • Contract-specific adjustments
  • Volume discounts and bundling impacts

Revenue Recognition Scheduling

Based on the performance obligations and price allocations, SAP RAR creates a detailed revenue recognition schedule. This automated process considers:

  1. Time-Based Recognition The software license and maintenance support revenues are scheduled over 36 months, with automatic recognition triggers at each period end.
  2. Event-Based Recognition Hardware revenue recognition is linked to delivery completion, while implementation revenue follows project milestone achievements.
  3. Multi-Element Considerations The system manages interdependencies between different performance obligations, ensuring proper revenue timing and allocation.

Financial Integration and Posting

The final stage involves the seamless integration with financial systems through a three-step process:

This robust process ensures that every revenue dollar is properly recognized, tracked, and reported according to the relevant accounting standards while maintaining complete audit trails and documentation.

Technical Architecture and Integration

At the heart of SAP RAR lies a sophisticated technical architecture that ensures seamless processing of revenue recognition requirements. The system integrates with various source systems through the Adapter Reuse Layer (ARL), which standardizes incoming data into Revenue Accounting Items (RAIs). These items then flow through the BRFplus rules engine, where business logic determines their treatment.

 

The Role of Artificial Intelligence

The integration of artificial intelligence in SAP RAR represents a significant leap forward in revenue recognition capabilities. Modern implementations leverage machine learning algorithms to analyze contract terms, predict revenue patterns, and identify potential recognition issues before they occur. Natural language processing capabilities help in automatically extracting key terms from contracts, while predictive analytics provide insights into future revenue trends.

Implementation Impact

Organizations implementing SAP RAR experience transformative benefits across their revenue operations. Manual processing time typically reduces by up to 80%, while recognition accuracy improves to near-perfect levels. The system's ability to handle complex revenue scenarios while maintaining compliance with IFRS 15 and ASC 606 provides organizations with confidence in their financial reporting.

Future Outlook

The future of revenue recognition in SAP RAR is closely tied to the advancement of intelligent technologies. As artificial intelligence and machine learning capabilities continue to evolve, we can expect even more sophisticated features in contract analysis, revenue prediction, and compliance automation. The system's ability to learn from historical patterns and adapt to new business scenarios will become increasingly valuable for organizations managing complex revenue streams.

Conclusion

SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting represents more than just a technical solution; it embodies a modern approach to revenue management that combines automation, intelligence, and compliance. As businesses continue to evolve with new revenue models and complex contracts, SAP RAR's robust foundation and forward-looking capabilities ensure that organizations can confidently manage their revenue recognition processes both today and in the future.

The next chapter discusses the Business AI component built on SAP BTP services to make RAR fully autonomous. Don’t forget to check it out! 😎
Part 2: Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) wit... - SAP Community

#SAP #RevenueRecognition #SAPRAR #IFRS15 #ASC606 #FinanceAutomation #RevenueManagement #ArtificialIntelligence #MachineLearning #Compliance #AccountingStandards #TechInnovation #BusinessEfficiency #DigitalTransformation #FinancialReporting #Automation #DataDriven #FutureOfFinance #ContractManagement #PerformanceObligations #AIinFinance #SAPSolutions #FinancialCompliance #RevenueAutomation #TechServeSolutions

entry.title: New Contract Item Line Remains In Preparation Status Instead of In Process
entry.content:
The contract was released and now In Process status. We added new contract Items into this approved contract and found the new items are In Preparation Status instead of In Process.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: New Contract Item Line Remains In Preparation Status...
entry.content:
The new item added in an existing contract is In Preparation status until it is released. Once released, then the item status changed to In Process. See screenshot attached.

In case of approval process enabled, it is the same. When a new item added, the user has to release the item by clicking release button. System then triggers the approval process. Once the approval is done, the line is then released, and status updated to In Process.

entry.title: Re: 'Unexpected Failure' error when publishing back to EC...
entry.content:
Hi @fransvanderme 

Had a chat with our Compensation Product Team today and they know about this issue for which a fix was deployed over the past days.

 

Could you please try again with a newly promoted employee to check if you can replicate ?

If you can, please create a ticket with our support team and share the number here with us.

All the best,
Xavier

entry.title: Re: Unable to create text id in sales order header and it...
entry.content:
Hello @AshishTumpally

Yes, in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can define text ID for the relevant document types.

Please follow the steps below in the IMG activities:

  • For Sales Document Header: Navigate to IMG -> Sales -> Sales Business Portfolio -> Sell from Stock -> Sell Products from Your Own Stock -> Define Text Types for SD Document Header.
  • For Sales Document Item: Navigate to IMG -> Sales -> Sales Business Portfolio -> Sell from Stock -> Sell Products from Your Own Stock -> Define Text Types for SD Document Item.

After defining the text IDs in this path, you can configure text determination for the relevant documents and select the relevant text ID Wherever necessary, based on your business requirements

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Post a 501 movement type using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content: I mapped the material, plant, and storage location to the other fields in the item table instead of the Receiving fields and now it works.

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA iDoc CREDAT and CRETIM format changes sending via ALE
entry.content: All our Outbound iDocs when downloaded thru WE19 as flat file shows CREDAT and CRETIM in YYYYMMDD and HHMMSS format. However, when they are sent to our customer, they received it in YYYY-MM-DD and HH:MM:SS format. We are doing ALE connection when doing the transfer. Is there anything that we need to check? Appreciate you advise. Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinars for new customers
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Schedule for February

RegionLanguageDate/TimeRegistration
APJEnglish4 Feb 2025 9:00 AM ISTRegister Now
EMEA/AmericasEnglish4 Feb 2025 3:00 PM CETRegister Now
South KoreaKorean20 Feb 2025 2:00 PM KSTRegister Now

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to start using the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Choose the time slot that works best for you and your team to attend.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SAP Cloud ALM helps you to implement and operate intelligent cloud and hybrid business solutions.

You benefit from an out-of-the-box, native cloud solution, designed as the central entry point to manage your SAP landscape with content-driven guided implementation and highly automated operations.

SAP Cloud ALM is included in your cloud subscription containing Enterprise Support, cloud edition and in SAP Enterprise Support. 

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

also…

is there a way to intentionally make it fail?

 

Please tell me

entry.title: Re: Mass change of equipment user status
entry.content: Hi Vaibhav , Thanks for your reply . Is it not possible todo through LSMW program .

entry.title: Re: Bonus Calculation for employee with Concurrent Employ...
entry.content:
Hi @MeghaSadhotra 

Unfortunately there is no mix and match in Compensation nor in Variable Pay in regards to Concurrent Employment or Global Assignment EC features.

Planning / Bonus calculation is either made using the Concurrent Employment record or using the Regular employment. 

To meet this customer requirement you will need to use custom workarounds that will involve integration center and MDF objects. 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: HANA CDS views using Visual Studio Code app by connecting to HANA db on Cloud...
entry.content:
Hello All,

Is it possible to create HANA CDS views (not ABAP CDS views) based on existing tables within the HANA db by connecting to HANA db on Cloud?

These are the things I explored so far in my attempt to created HANA CDS views from existing HANA db tables on Cloud

1. I was not able to connect to the HANA db using the Business Application Studio on BTP because for some reason the HANA db driver extension is not available there. Did anyone encounter such issue and what to do

2.I was able to connect to the HANA db on Cloud using VS Code

I have not done this before and I have been exploring ways for creating HANA CDS views from existing HANA db tables in VS Code

I installed extensions for HANA db explorer, CDS language support but could not find much in terms of tutorials as to how to go about connecting to the hdb and creating a CDS

Could anyone please give some directions on this?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Using Sets in ABAP Program
entry.content:
A set is a flexible structure for organizing hierarchies and quantities.
You can group values or value intervals in a set and several sets into a hierarchy.
Values in sets refer to a dimension of a database table.
Tcode - GS01

entry.title: Streamlining PDF Transfers to Ariba with SAP CPI: Solving Multipart Form-Data Formatting Challenges
entry.content:
In this blog post, we explore the challenge of sending a PDF file in binary format to the Ariba 'create-project-document' API or any other third party system using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI). A common issue arises when using the Content Modifier to handle data in multipart form-data format, as this can disturb the PDF format.

To address this, implementing a Groovy script provides an effective solution. This script constructs the multipart form-data payload correctly for file transmission without altering the PDF's integrity.

 

The Challenge:

When attempting to send a PDF file using the Content Modifier in multipart form-data format, users often find that the PDF format gets distorted, resulting in unsuccessful data transmission. The Content Modifier, while useful for many tasks, can inadvertently alter binary data, leading to corrupted files.

The Solution:

Using a Groovy script within SAP CPI, we can properly format the PDF data as multipart form-data. Here's a Groovy script example that can be used to achieve this:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

import java.util.HashMap;

def Message processData(Message message) {

def pdfContent = message.getProperty("pdfFileContent") as byte[];

def boundary = "----WebKitFormBoundary7MA4YWxkTrZu0gW";

 

def payload = new StringBuilder();

payload.append("--").append(boundary).append(" ");

payload.append("Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="document.pdf" ");

payload.append("Content-Type: application/pdf ");

payload.append(new String(pdfContent, StandardCharsets.ISO_8859_1)).append(" ");

payload.append("--").append(boundary).append("--");

 

message.setHeader("Content-Type", "multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary);

message.setBody(payload.toString().getBytes(StandardCharsets.ISO_8859_1));

return message;

}

 

Additional Tip:

When adding the PDF binary data to a property using a Content Modifier, it’s essential not to set any data type. Instead, start by adding this Groovy script to convert the InputStream to a byte array:

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

import java.util.HashMap;

def Message processData(Message message) {

def body = message.getBody(java.io.InputStream);

 

// Convert InputStream to byte array

byte[] byteArray = body.bytes;

 

// Set the byte array as the new message body

message.setBody(byteArray);

 

return message;

}

Thank you for reading this blog! If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to ask in the comments section below.

This blog post should help other SAP CPI users understand the issue with multipart form-data and how to resolve it using Groovy scripts.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: How to sign PDF documents
entry.content:
Some time ago I needed to sign a PDF document in SAP Integration Suite; I looked at the signer standard elements and I missed a PDF Signer…

I supposed that it needs a commercial library to sign PDFs (like iText) and maybe it is not as easy as I thought…

So, finally, I needed to implement in a Groovy Script the functionality to sign the PDFs documents. Here I share some information that I founded very useful:

  • First, you need to download the iText libraries in the version you need (https://itextpdf.com/resources/downloads). Take in account that for commercial use you need the appropriate license.
  • In my case, I imported the iText libraries directly in my iflow and I implemented a Groovy script, but you can find easier to work in Java and export the code as a custom jar, for example.
  • The private key is the “key” of the solution. To sign the PDF you need to recover the private key and the certificates stored in the SAP Integration Suite Keystore. It is possible using these libraries:
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory;
import com.sap.it.api.keystore.KeystoreService;

KeystoreService service = ITApiFactory.getApi(KeystoreService.class, null);
Certificate cert = service.getCertificate(keystore_alias);
Certificate[] certChain = service.getCertificateChain(keystore_alias);
KeyPair keypair = service.getKeyPair(keystore_alias);
PrivateKey privateKey = keypair.getPrivate();
  • With the private key and the certificates, you can implement your code to sign the document with iText. There are many examples in Internet, and it depends on the version chosen. As an example:
PrivateKeySignature privateKeySignature= new PrivateKeySignature(privateKey, "SHA-256", "IAIK");

if(privateKeySignature!=null) {
        BouncyCastleDigest bouncyCastleDigest = new BouncyCastleDigest();
        MakeSignature.signDetached(appearance, (ExternalDigest)bouncyCastleDigest, (ExternalSignature)privateKeySignature, certChain, null, null, null, 0, MakeSignature.CryptoStandard.CMS);
}

And here it is my signed PDF:

Finally, using iText you can change the appearance of the signature in many ways, for example:

    PdfSignatureAppearance appearance = stamper.getSignatureAppearance();

    Integer pageNumber = 1;
    Rectangle rect=new Rectangle(50,100,220,140);
    appearance.setAcro6Layers(false);
    appearance.setLayer4Text(PdfSignatureAppearance.questionMark);
    appearance.setVisibleSignature(rect,pageNumber, "sig2");

Hope it will be helpful!

entry.title: Re: Handling Date Logic in SAC and SAP BW
entry.content:
Hi @Jan-Nicolas 

There are multiple approaches we can use.

1. By using customer exits variable.(Recommended).

Use the customer exit variables to provide the required month as a filter or calculated Field 

2.By creating calculated column in SAC

3. By using custom calendar in BW and use that as time-date dimension in SAC 

For deviation 

1. You can create calculated Field in BW on the bases of customer exits.

2.in SAC you can calculate the date difference. As well as variance for previous year values 

Hope this will help 

Let me know if you need further information.

Thanks

entry.title: For valuation area 1000 is not exactly one logistic curtype defined.
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

In S4hana ,I had created a new plant 1000, under which i am not able to maintain the material master "Accounting1" view getting an error- For valuation area 1000 is not exactly one logistic curtype defined.

 

Kindly Help me to resolve the issue.

 

Thanks in Advance.

Saikaat Saha

entry.title: Two fields using WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION always using one of the field as basis in navigation
entry.content:
I've developed a fiori app using cds annotation. It has two fields which are accounting document and a reclass accounting document.

I've added an WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION on both fields. When I click on the accounting document value it goes to the manage journal entries as expected but when I click on the reclass accounting document, in goes into manage journal entries but the value its taking is the accounting document column and not the reclass.

How can I make the navigation know which field to take to be used when navigating to another app?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
We are excited to announce significant enhancements in Post Upgrade Testing (PUT) with the 2502 release of the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. Key features include:

New Post-Upgrade Test Application

  • A new PUT app with an intuitive dashboard for clear test execution metrics.
  • One-click navigation between PUT-related applications.
  • Advanced comparison capabilities for different PUT runs.
  • Enhanced contextual information for triggering and tracking PUT executions.
  • Consolidated access to essential PUT actions like consent management and role maintenance.

Intelligent Test Scoper(i-TS) App Enhancements:

  • Improved analytics within the Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) app for optimized scope efficiency.

These enhancements aim to provide more efficient, user-friendly applications that facilitate smooth upgrades of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment.

PUT App - Dashboard view 

PUT Results Comparator

The PUT results comparator enables detailed analysis between different PUT runs. Key features include:

  • · Compare execution variants within the same release or across different releases.
  • · View results side-by-side for easy comparison.
  • · Navigate to detailed logs of both failed and successful runs.

Trigger and Track PUT - Analytics

Post Upgrade Tests are triggered via the Trigger functionality and latest executions can be tracked via the Track functionality. Along with the Post Upgrade Test Execution summary, the following information can be viewed for the latest run.

  • Execution scope
  • Pre check status
  • Execution distribution with and without TDC
  • Distribution of test processes based on failure categories

Consent Management for Post Upgrade Tests

Enhanced user experience with the new change history feature introduced where the user can track consent change information such as 

  • Release information
  • Consent changed by
  • Consent changed on

Please note: For a new customer the PUT Consent will be set to "Yes" by default.

Intelligent Test Scoper(i-TS) App Enhancements 

End users will be able to quickly check the efficiency gains from utilizing the optimized test scope for PUT. 
A more detailed application area-wise optimization gains is also provided.


SAP Delivered Standard Automates

The total list of SAP delivered automated test scripts are 448 for 2502 and can be accessed via link – Automated Test Scripts Index”. This includes 5 new scope items which were added this release.

Further detailed change information about the standard automate is available in the “Automated Test Script Change History” and “Test Script Release Change Information”.

Important Links and Updates

We have created a new Learning Hub course for an overview of the tool. This is a good starting point for anyone who is new to the tool or want to get an overview of the new features we have added over the past couple of years. [Before you can access the link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guidance can be found here]

As part of our PUT 2.0 Enablement also we have created a set of short videos which cover the most important aspects of the process. The video playlist can be accessed here.

Links to other important blogs:

  • How to get started with the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release by Bert Schulze

Please bookmark the test automation community page to keep up with the latest updates happening in the test automation space.

For more details on the functionality please refer the links below:

Post-Upgrade Tests App
Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) 
Manage Upgrade Tests 
Manage Your Test Processes
Test Your Processes
Test Data Container
Analyze Automated Test Results
Configure Conditional Authentication
Also refer FAQs

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn.

🎥Watch the Replay of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.

🔍Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Issue with DataHub Deployment: PayerID Not Displaying in Production
entry.content:
Description:

I am experiencing an issue with the DataHub configuration when deploying the jar in the Production environment. Below is a detailed explanation of the problem:

  1. UAT Environment:

    • When I deployed the jar in the UAT environment, everything worked perfectly.
    • The PayerID attribute was correctly displayed based on the configuration.

    • Production Environment:

      • After deploying the same jar in the Production environment, the PayerID is not displaying.

      • Jar Comparison:

        • I have compared the jars deployed in both UAT and Production environments, and they are identical.

          Configuration Details:

          Raw:

          <item>
          <type>CanonicalPartyRelationshipSales</type>
          <description>Canonical representation of a relationship between two parties</description>
          <attributes>
          <attribute>
          <name>relationshipTypeCP</name>
          <transformations>
          <transformation>
          <rawSource>RawDEBMAS</rawSource>
          <expression>E1KNA1M-E1KNVVM-E1KNVPM-PARVW</expression>
          </transformation>
          </transformations>
          </attribute>
          <attribute>
          <name>businessCustomerNumber</name>
          <transformations>
          <transformation>
          <rawSource>RawDEBMAS</rawSource>
          <expression>E1KNA1M-E1KNVVM-E1KNVPM-KUNN2</expression>
          </transformation>
          </transformations>
          </attribute>
          </attributes>
          </item>

           

          Canonical:

          <item>
          <type>CanonicalPartyRelationshipSales</type>
          <attributes>
          <attribute>
          <name>relationshipTypeCP</name>
          <model>
          <localizable>false</localizable>
          <collection>false</collection>
          <type>String</type>
          <primaryKey>false</primaryKey>
          </model>
          </attribute>
          <attribute>
          <name>businessCustomerNumber</name>
          <model>
          <localizable>false</localizable>
          <collection>false</collection>
          <type>String</type>
          <primaryKey>false</primaryKey>
          </model>
          </attribute>
          </attributes>
          </item>

           

          Target:

          <item>
          <type>CanonicalPartyReB2BUnit</type>
          <exportCode>B2BUnit</exportCode>
          <description>Update payerId</description>
          <canonicalItemSource>CanonicalPartyRelationshipSales</canonicalItemSource>
          <updatable>true</updatable>
          <filterExpression>'RE'.equals(relationshipTypeCP)</filterExpression>
          <attributes>
          <attribute>
          <name>uid</name>
          <transformationExpression>resolve('CanonicalParty').externalPartyId.concat('_').concat(salesKey)</transformationExpression>
          <exportCode>uid[unique=true]</exportCode>
          <mandatoryInHeader>true</mandatoryInHeader>
          </attribute>
          <attribute>
          <name>payerId</name>
          <transformationExpression>businessCustomerNumber</transformationExpression>
          <exportCode>payerId</exportCode>
          </attribute>
          </attributes>
          </item>

          Observed Issue:

          • The filterExpression ('RE'.equals(relationshipTypeCP)) works as expected in UAT, and the payerId attribute appears. However, in Production, the payerId is not being displayed despite having the same jar configuration.

entry.title: Diffrence in APi versions of S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

 

we are exploring the public cloud APIs.

 

Can you pls let me know the difference between 
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for ABAP Platform

Vs

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Diffrence in APi versions of S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Developer Extensibility is available to the public as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP environment. It reuses the development model from the SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ABAP Environment offers SAP customers and partners the opportunity to build tightly coupled extensions running directly on the S/4HANA Cloud technology stack. This new extensibility option complements the existing key user extensibility and side-by side extensibility options. The below community helps you to connect to peers and SAP experts to exchange information about SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment, search for answers, ask questions, access resources, and more.

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud-abap-environment

You can find more information on this below.

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/develop-extensions-using-sap-s-4hana-cloud-abap-environment/introducing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-abap-environment_cd34bb5f-58fe-408a-840d-b9af40b77f66

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4036/4243/

https://api.sap.com/package/S4HANACloudABAPPlatform/overview


Basically you have Embedded steampunk along with the cloud product to build more custom developments for the ABAP platform.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content:
@VaradBhagwat The second 2 screenshots are hard to read.

1) Where are you sending the credentials?

2) We usually solve CORS by creating a proxy service, or more simply using BTP destinations

entry.title: Re: How to install SAP NetWeaver RFC SDK 7.50
entry.content: same, unable to download from the link

entry.title: Re: Inventory should be blocked at the delivery and not at picking
entry.content: Hi Antonio, can you spend a few words on the blocking aspect? Do you mean that the stock is 'reserved' such that concurrent demand cannot consume it? This should already be possible by considering deliveries in the scope of check. Usually it is decided late in the process which batch to choose (e. g. due to warehouse efficiency). What is the business reason the customer/user need to force the batch in the sales order already?

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Scheduled Custom Widget
entry.content:
Hi 

You can use export to excel API in SAC and in BTP you can use email scheduler or Build RPA workflow to sent that file to all the receipants 

You can also use REST API but in that I think we can not maintain the exact formating of table.

Let me know if you need further information.

Regards 

Bilal

entry.title: Re: Ariba Network 2Q21 Release: FAQ for Ariba Network becoming part of SAP Business Network
entry.content: Is the SBN available inside KSA now?

entry.title: Re: unable to visualize Heterogenous graph in sap hana ....
entry.content: As of Q1 2025, heterogenous property graphs are not supported by the Graph Viewer in Database Explorer.

entry.title: Re: Ariba Network 2Q21 Release: FAQ for Ariba Network becoming part of SAP Business Network
entry.content:
SAP Business Network, to be Hosted in Saudi Arabia, Will Expand Local and Global Trade Opportunities - SAP Africa News Center

Any confirmation on this ?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA iDoc CREDAT and CRETIM format changes sending via ALE
entry.content: I guess you have to tell us a bit more about your scenario. When you talk about WE19 isn't it the transaction to upload IDOCs for test purpose? What kind of communication do you have for your outbound IDOC? It's really "ALE" - mean you send it to another SAP System or is here a middleware involved? Which kind of outbound port you have used - maybe you can share a screenshot of it - you find it in WE21.

entry.title: Adding email body content for F.27
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to add mail body content for program RFKORK00 (tcode f.27). the user exit that i have found is EXIT_RFKORIEX_001. After adding, seems like the body content is not shown in the mail. Any suggestion? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Core hybrid New hire Replication: IT 0002 First record start date issue
entry.content:
Hello, 

I create this badi to replicate infotype 0002 with begda = birth day. 

when I try to replicatE an employee with new employment ( he has 2 userid, 2 pernr), I get this issue : 

"Change in earliest retroactive accounting for time evaluation not possible"

any help?

entry.title: Get BP contact address details with API_BUSINESS_PARTNER
entry.content:
BP DIGITS has contact PHONE_NUMBER with an address with all the details (e-mail, fax, telephone, mobile phone; see attached document). We cannot find a way to retrieve these details with REST API API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.


 


We are calling GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BPContactToAddress?$filter=BusinessPartnerCompany eq 'DIGITS'&$expand=to_EmailAddress,to_FaxNumber,to_MobilePhoneNumber,to_PhoneNumber


But in the response we just receive empty arrays:


"to_EmailAddress": {
                    "results": []
                },
                "to_FaxNumber": {
                    "results": []
                },
                "to_MobilePhoneNumber": {
                    "results": []
                },
                "to_PhoneNumber": {
                    "results": []
                },


Can you instruct us what is the right way to retrieve the data?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Get BP contact address details with API_BUSINESS_PARTNER
entry.content:
Please try the url:
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartnerContact(RelationshipNumber='xxx',BusinessPartnerCompany='xxxxx',BusinessPartnerPerson='xxxx',ValidityEndDate=datetime'9999-12-31T00:00:00')/to_ContactRelationship?$format=json

entry.title: Can we Delete a Product ?
entry.content:
Can we Delete a Product using Manage Product Master Data Application ?

PLM (Product Lifecycle Management)

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Wien - 13.02.2025
entry.content:
---
Diesmal Semesterferien-bedingt um eine Woche verschoben und passend zum ersten Stammtisch im neuen Jahr in einer alt bewährten Location!
---

Ein gemütlicher Abend in lockerer Runde - zum zwanglosen Austausch über Themen rund um SAP und mehr.
Von der SAP Community, für die SAP Community

Tritt der Gruppe Wien bei und subscribe den Blog-Post SAP Stammtisch Wien, um über zukünftige Events informiert zu werden!

entry.title: Re: Equipment installation to Functional location does no...
entry.content:
Hi adrakev,

First things first, make sure you got the prerequisites in place:

1. Configuration (I assume u did already)

2. Serialization of the Equipment. 
If the movement like installation (261 mvmt type) has to happen in ur SAP, this Equipment has to be serialized. It means it needs a serial number, which links Material and Equipment. 

3. Access to transaction IE4 

Via this transaction (once properly configured) you are able to do installation/dismantling of Serialized Equipment. The effect is as follows: 

- both, structural installation and goods movement (261) will be triggered in the background. For this kind of activity you will also need a reference - make sure you have a PM Order for example ‘Installation of a pump’. 

Cheers,

Dan

entry.title: No recipient found for message type PORDCH in the ALE modelMessage No. B1129
entry.content:
No recipient found for message type PORDCH in the ALE model Message No. B1129

I am getting this error, While Triggering outbound IDOC via output determination for PO change scenario. I have maintained sender and receiver system in BD64. Message control used in we20 settings also

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Bank Master data in Test environment...
entry.content: Hi Edrilan, Yes we have assigned users SAP_BR_Cash_Manager role assigned.

entry.title: Opportunity Management in S/4HANA Customer Management and CRM
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA CM's opportunity management enhances sales team performance while improving decision-making processes. Offering an integrated structure, it works seamlessly with various SAP modules, enabling business processes to progress more quickly and effectively.

Its customizable nature allows SAP S/4HANA CM opportunity management to meet the needs of different industries, providing businesses with both a competitive edge and long-term customer satisfaction. This system forms a solid foundation for sustainable growth by delivering cost and time savings.

Opportunity documents are primarily created to acquire customers, making them typically targeted at leads. A lead is defined as a person or business that has not yet become a customer of an organization but has the potential to be interested in its products or services. Leads are managed through roles and are registered in the system with the "Lead" role. If the opportunity is won, they are recorded as an actual customer.

 

Features

SAP CRM Interface:

  • User-friendly graphical interface.
  • Create, update, and track opportunities.

 

Status and Stage Management:

  • Monitor the current status of opportunities (e.g., open, won, lost).
  • Plan actions specific to development stages.

 

Document and Note Management: 

  • Save opportunity-related documents and notes in the system.
  • Allow team members to share information.

 

Activity Management:

  • Track and manage activities such as meetings, calls, and emails.

 

Analytics and Reporting: 

  • Generate detailed sales performance reports.
  • Integration with SAP BW and SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

Automated Workflows: 

  • Trigger-based automation (e.g., proposal or order creation).
  • Notifications and task assignments.

 

Advantages

Real-Time Data: 

  • Fast and accurate information flow.

 

Forecast Accuracy: 

  • More effective analyses.

 

Increased Efficiency: 

  • Time savings and process optimization.

 

Ease of Use: 

  • Simple and flexible user interface.

 

Deep Integration: 

  • Compatibility with SAP ECC, S/4HANA, and other SAP modules.

 

Customer Satisfaction: 

  • Improved service delivery and process tracking.

 

 

 

Adaptations

 

Sales Cycle and Sales Stages

Sales Cycle

The Sales Cycle encompasses all the steps from the beginning to the end of a sales process. This includes the initial contact with a customer, identifying opportunities, preparing proposals, making the deal, and completing the sale thereafter. SAP S/4HANA CM (Customer Management) provides various tools and modules to track and manage this process. The sales cycle is typically defined as an opportunity management process and includes all customer interactions.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Define Sales Cycle:

 

Sales Stage

A Sales Stage refers to smaller, specific phases within the sales cycle. These stages represent different steps in the progression of the sale, with each stage aimed at achieving a particular goal or outcome. For example, a sales opportunity may first move to the "New" stage, then progress through stages such as "Quote Given," "In Negotiation," and "Closed-Won." Each stage includes actions taken at that point and tasks to be followed up on.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Define Sales Stages:

 

Assign Sales Stages to Sales Cycles

Assigning sales stages to sales cycles determines the stages a sales opportunity progresses through as it moves through a specific sales cycle, and defines the probability of each stage. In SAP CRM, the processes related to how sales stages are assigned to sales cycles and how they are structured can be summarized as follows:

  1. Defining Sales Stages:
    In this phase, you define which sales stages will be present in each sales cycle. For example, a sales cycle may include sales stages such as "Opportunity Identification", "Proposal Preparation" and "Initial Contact".                                                                  
  2. Setting the Time Sequence:
    In this step, you specify the sequence of sales stages within the sales cycle. This order determines the progression of the opportunity through various stages until it reaches the final outcome. For example, the "Proposal Preparation" stage comes after the "Opportunity Identification" stage.                                                                                   
  3. Setting the Probability:
    The success probability is defined for each sales stage. For example, the likelihood of success during the information exchange stage of an opportunity could be 30%. This probability represents the chance of closing the opportunity at that particular stage.        
  4. Default Success Probability Settings:
    SAP transfers the success probabilities you have defined as default values to opportunities. For example, when an opportunity progresses to a specific stage, the success probability of that stage is automatically added to the opportunity.

 

NOTE - Value 0 is not transferred as the default: 

If you don't want the success probability of a sales stage to be set to 0, you can configure SAP to not transfer this value by default. To do this, you should make the following configuration in the SMOFPARSFA table for the CRMOPPORT parameter:

- Parameter Name: SALES_STAGE

- Parameter Value: NO_DEFAULT_PROB_ZERO

- Value: X

This setting ensures that a success probability of 0 is not automatically applied to new opportunities.  

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Assign Sales Stages to Sales Cycles:

 

Assignment of Sales Cycle to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Define Transaction Types, after following these steps, the relevant sales cycle is defined within the opportunity document type on the page that appears. The sales stages associated with the sales cycle are displayed within the document, allowing you to track the progression of the opportunity through the defined stages.

 

Sales Stage History

The changes in the sales stage within the document can be viewed from the "Sales Stage History" tab on the web UI screen.

 

Define Opportunity Group and Source

Define Opportunity Group

The opportunity group is used as a classification feature for opportunities and can also be used in analyses. This allows different business areas or company initiatives to be viewed at a global level. Depending on the selected transaction type, the relevant opportunity group is automatically assigned to the document.

For example, if your company operates on a global scale and the business processes in the U.S. differ from those in Europe, all opportunities belonging to the "Europe" group can be reviewed in a single assessment.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Opportunity Group and Origin > Define Opportunity Group:

 

Assignment of Opportunity Group to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Define Transaction Types, after following these steps, the relevant opportunity group is defined within the opportunity document type on the page that appears. The opportunity group is set as the default for the created document.

 

Define Source for Opportunities

Defining the source for opportunities is used to record where the opportunities originate from on the document. For example, a source can be defined as a trade fair or a phone call. This way, the revenue generated from trade fairs or sales agreements can be analyzed.

Opportunities can be grouped by sources such as trade fairs, phone calls, or website interactions, and the impact of each source on the opportunities can be analyzed.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Opportunity Group and Origin > Define Source for Opportunities:

 

Status Reason for Opportunity

The reason for the opportunity status and status schema are important for the evaluation and analysis of opportunities. A reason explaining the result (won, lost) of an opportunity must be documented and assessed. The reason for the status changes depending on the current state of the opportunity.

Example:

  • Status: Won
  • Reason: Due to the best product

In this process, a flexible set of reasons can be defined according to the opportunity statuses using a catalog. This enables a more detailed analysis of why opportunities were won or lost.

 

Status Reason for Opportunity

The reason for the opportunity status is actually similar to the category creation process. First, a catalog of type "H Status Reason (Opportunities)" is created.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Catalogs:

  

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Groups and Codes for Catalogs, After following these steps, the created catalog is selected on the page that appears, and a code group is created.

 

The reasons that should appear in the document are added to the code group.

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Group Profiles, After following these steps, a code group profile is created by specifying the catalog, code group, and code on the page that appears.

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Group Profiles, After following these steps, details such as the code group profile and code groups are specified on the page that appears, and a status code profile is created.

 

Assignment of Status Reason to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Assign Status Profile and Subject Profile to Business Transaction Type, After following these steps, status reasons are assigned to the document on the page that appears. This screen includes functionalities such as:

  • Listing reasons based on statuses,
  • Defining whether reason selection is mandatory or optional.

 

Creating a Sales Order from an Opportunity Document

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Copy Control for Opportunities and Sales Transactions > Copying of Transaction Types:

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Copy Control for Opportunities and Sales Transactions > Copying of Item Categories:

 

Creating Interlinked Opportunity Documents

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Copying Control for Opportunity Item to Opportunity:

 

Surveys Linked to Opportunity Documents

The "CRM_SURVEY_SUITE" transaction code or the web UI screen is used to access the survey editor application.

 

A survey tailored to the request and needs is prepared and activated.

 

Defining Conditions for Surveys

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Sales Methodology > Questionnaire for Opportunities, After following these steps, criteria are defined on the page that determines under what conditions the surveys will be used. These criteria are as follows:

  • Status
  • Priority
  • Source
  • Opportunity Group
  • Sales Stage
  • Organization
  • Distribution Channel
  • Department
  • Expected Sales Revenue
  • Expected Total Value
  • Currency 

 

Assigning Surveys to Opportunity Documents

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Sales Methodology > Define Determination for Questionnaires, After following these steps, the survey type is linked to the document type on the page that appears. Additionally, criteria groups, mandatory status, and validity information can also be defined.

 

Competitors

In this field, you can define which of the following you want to display on the opportunity overview page:

  • If you leave the selection blank, you can display competitors without products at header and item level.
  • If you choose Header Mode, you can display competitors with products at header level.

    In this case, the competitors and competitor products refer to the entire opportunity, not to individual items.

  • If you choose Item Mode, you can display competitors with products at item level.

    In this case, the competitors and competitor products refer to only one item, that is, you can define the corresponding competitors and competitor products separately for each item in your opportunity.

If "Header Mode" is selected:

If "Item Mode" is selected:

If the competitor mode is not selected:

 

Creating a Competitor Product

Cross-Application Components > Master Data Synchronization > Customer/Vendor Integration > Settings for Customer Integration > Field Assignment for Customer Integration > Assign Keys > Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Customer(Table: TBD001), On the next page, the account group is matched with the business partner group. The account group should be '0006' for competitors.

 

A partner is created in the competitor group. The customer number is entered according to the appropriate number range defined in the FLCU01 role.

 

A material of type VVGR is created using the transaction code “MM01”.

 

The customer number is entered for the material using the transaction code "MM02".

 

Search Competitor Product:

 

Competitor Page:

 

Tables Related to Opportunities

  • CRMC_OPPIMPOR: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority
  • CRMC_OPPIMPOR_T: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority: Text
  • CRMC_OPPORT_H: Transaction Type - Opportunity Control Parameters
  • CRMC_OPPT_OD: Customization Table for Transaction Types (Opportunity)
  • CRMC_OPPT_TYPE: Opportunity Type
  • CRMC_OPPT_TYPE_T: Opportunity Types: Texts
  • CRMC_SVY_OPPT: Define Criteria for Surveys
  • CRMC_SVY_OPPT_T: Define Criteria for Surveys: Text
  • CRMD_DH_DATA_OPP: Opportunity Data Buffer
  • CRMD_OPP_LINK: Opportunity Linkages (Main Records)
  • CRMD_OPPORT_H: Opportunity
  • CRMD_OPPORT_I: Opportunity Item Table
  • CRMD_PPM_OPPBUF: Real-Time Buffer Fields for All Opportunities
  • CRMD_PPM_OPPBUFI: Real-Time Buffer Fields for All Opportunities (Item Level)
  • CRMS4_LCOPPTSTAT: Opportunity Life Cycle Status
  • CRMS4_OPPTASRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Search
  • CRMS4_OPPTHSRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Header Data
  • CRMS4_OPPTIMPOR: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority
  • CRMS4_OPPTISRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Item
  • CRMS4_OPPTSEARCH: Opportunity / Prospect Search
  • CRMS4D_OPPT_H: Opportunity and Prospect Header Details
  • CRMS4D_OPPT_I: Opportunity and Prospect Item Details
  • CRMS4V_SH_OPPT_H: Documents by Object Number
  • CRMV_OPPIMPOR: Opportunity / Prospect Priority
  • CRMV_OPPORT_H: Opportunity Customization at Header Level
  • CRMV_OPPORT_ITEM: Opportunity Customization at Item Level
  • CRMV_OPPT_OD: Transaction Types for Opportunity OData Service
  • CRMV_OPPT_TYPE: Opportunity Group
  • CRMV_SVY_DET_OPPT: Define Survey Criteria (Opportunity)
  • CRMV_SVY_OPPT: Define Survey Criteria for Opportunities

     

 

Restrictions with S/4HANA CM

The following functionalities are not supported in Opportunity Management:

  1. Purchasing Organization: It is not possible to define a purchasing organization at the account level and use it as default in the opportunity. Additionally, the graphical representation of the purchasing organization is no longer supported.
  2. Pipeline Management (PPM).
  3. Limitations on Competitor Product Relationships.
  4. Configurable Items.

entry.title: Re: Creating Roles and Managing Authorizations in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps does not currently have role-based access, except to VCF. Some possible ideas:

  • If you want to use BTP roles, you could create a CAP service that defines roles, assign those roles to users as you would with any other roles in the cockpit, and then when your app loads see the roles and hide the UIs as needed. This is described in this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-cap-app.html#0bb3011a-41c3-40dc-90d3-5084d2fab47b
  • You can use the role based access in VCF to allow an admin to manage a table(s) with user permissions, and then similarly check these when a user opens the app.

These are just my ideas, and I do not know what would be the recommendation by the product team.

entry.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service: Removing property enable-xsuaa-support
entry.content:
Maybe you are wondering why the property "enable-xsuaa-support" was removed from the Job Scheduling service documentation and blog post. Wonder no more.

"enable-xsuaa-support: true" was removed from the instance creation process as it's now considered true for all instances.

You can also check the "What's new" section in the official documentation:

Updated Procedure for Creating a Service Instance  
You no longer need to specify the enable-xsuaa-support: true property when creating a service instance using the SAP BTP cockpit or the CF CLI. It is now set by default.  
Info only
Changed	2024-11-14

But what this means?

Your instance of the Job Scheduling service is configured to work with XSUAA out-of-the-box. It's secure by default.

Do I have to do anything?

No, your existing instances are fine; you don't have to do anything.

Can I set enable-xsuaa-support: false

You can pass this parameter when creating a service instance, but nothing will happen - it will just be ignored.

Creating a Service Instance

Now what will be the difference in creating service instance?

Just skip the parameters.

BTP Cockpit

For example on Step 1 (New Instance or Subscription) after filling the required data you can just select "Finish"

 

Or if you go to step 2 (Parameters) you can leave it empty and continue.

 

CF CLI

Or with the CF CLI (Cloud Foundry Command Line Interface). See Help Portal for reference.

cf create-service jobscheduler standard my-scheduler

Creating service instance my-scheduler in org <org-name> / space denis as <mail>...

Service instance my-scheduler created.
OK

entry.title: When is the bank charge posted in payment process
entry.content:
Dear community,

I have a question about the bank charge posting in S4 HANA public edition. Can I configure it to be posted in the automatic payment run F110?

According to the official document, the bank charge seems to be posted when a DME file is created. However, I confirmed it when a payment file from a bank is imported instead. 

Thanks.

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAC - How in the filter can I only show the members, a user is authorized to see
entry.content: Facing the same issue. Need to filter list of members on dimension filter based on user authorization. Is there any way to achieve that?

entry.title: Re: Opportunity Management in S/4HANA Customer Management and CRM
entry.content: Very helpful ! Thanx Serkan.

entry.title: Re: Asset Impairment / Asset Revaluation Configuration - SAP S4Hana
entry.content:
Hello, 

Thank you for this valuable post. My question is: what if instead of revaluation of acquisition cost we want for the impairment value to be deducted from the Net Book Value of the asset ?

entry.title: Re: How to convert SAP WebDynpro Apps to FIORI apps with...
entry.content:
Hi Bhaskar,

Depending on the SAP_UI release you are using, you can make some adjustments to better align with the SAP Fiori Design and Product Standards when working with Classic UIs, such as those based on FPM/WDA and SAP_UI 758 (ABAP Platform 2023). Here are a few suggestions:

  1. Choose one of the modern SAP theme families: either sap_horizon* or sap_fiori_3*
  2. Incorporate Header UIBBs in the floorplans (OVP, OIF, GAF), such as the Search UIBB and Attributes Filter UIBB, see here
  3. Apply the Object Page Mode to the OVP floorplans (refer to this blog and SAP Help for more details)

You can check the outcome in one of the following test and demo applications:

  1. FPM_SADL_CRUD_TEST_EPM_SO
  2. FPM_SADL_CRUD_TEST_EPM_PD
  3. FPM_SADL_CRUD_TEST_EPM_PD_CTLG
  4. FPM_TEST_OVP_RESPONSIVE
  5. FPM_TEST_FORM_REP_DYNAMIC_162
  6. etc.

Best regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: MB52 Report send automatically to mail by batch job
entry.content: Hi, Thanks for the post it was very helpful. I just want to ask that can you send to you the whole MB52 list? Cause I just receive an 18 table .htm file extension and it is of course not the full report. Am I miss something? Thank you for your reply in advance!

entry.title: Re: disp-work, Dispatcher, GRAY, Stopped, , ,10102
entry.content:
Hello @mamartins,

thank you for your answer.

What exactly do I have to license, I have already licensed my SAP S/4 HANA 2023 system. The key is also valid until 04.02.2025.
Do I also have to license the HANA DB? If so, how exactly do I do that?
I cannot find a suitable description for an S/4 HANA DB in the SAP Portal under request license key.
The system is on-premise.

entry.title: Re: Sales Order is blocked, even although credit limit is...
entry.content: I have cross checked in FD32, there is no Next Inspection Date .

entry.title: Re: 2502 C4C Release Discussion
entry.content:
Hi Molly,

do we know where exactly it will be published?

Can you share the link ?

entry.title: Re: Trigger event lost in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Mio Yasutake,

Did you found a solution for this? We are trying the exact same thing and it doesn't work for us either in the Event Mesh, Integration Suite while in the other hand in the Event Mesh, Default Plan (with the same set-up) is working. 

Also the SBPA API trigger for the events that SAP states in the documentation is working from the postman but we only face problems in the Integration Suite, Event Mesh

Thank you very much in advance

entry.title: I want to batch update bank groups in the bank master.
entry.content:
I want to update the bank group in the bank master.
To update the registered bank master information in a batch, I think I will use "Import Bank Directories (BIC2S)".

In the application, I can choose the file template to upload, such as SWIFT or JBA.
I don't see the bank group in the template item, so I'm wondering if I can't update it.
I checked the Help Portal and it said I could update the bank group, but I didn't know what to do.
Could you give me some advice?
If you have any examples of how this works, I would appreciate it if you could comment.

entry.title: Re: Unlocking Efficiency: Leveraging SAP BTP Document Management Service in Fiori Applications # 1
entry.content: Is there any document to call DMS reuse UI component from Fiori application deployed in ABAP in BTP?

entry.title: Re: 2 Years Quarterly Historical Data Upload Approach
entry.content:
I would recommend not to do the reversals and stay with the periodic way of storing data in the product. GR is not designed to persist YTD data. Instead, YTD is calculated in analytics.

Hence, YTD data should be looked at in analytics by adding all data from period 0 to reporting quarter, e.g. Q3: per 0-9.

entry.title: Copy PO Item Text to GR Item Text
entry.content:
Is there a possible way to automate copying of PO Item text to GR Item text in MIGO via standard configuration?

As much as possible we would like to avoid using any changes with ABAP. We are currently using SAP ECC.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Standard Text Key in Fiori apps for maintenance orders
entry.content:
The standard text key can be added through transaction UIBB ( web Dynpro settings)

Following note contains details on the resolution:

3470624 - "Standard Text Key" for Operation is not available in WDA app - SAP for Me

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM awarness sessions 2025 - What is in it & upcoming schedule
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Unveiling the February Lineup: SAP Cloud ALM Awareness Sessions, we call this session "Maximize your SAP Investment through SAP Cloud ALM: Implementation & Operation"

Who should attend 

​Customers who are at the beginning of their SAP Cloud ALM journey with little to no experience in this area

Session Goal

​provide a first understanding of what SAP Cloud ALM offers, understand its significance, and explore ways to onboard

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP Cloud ALM
  • SAP Cloud ALM accelerates implementation
  • SAP Cloud ALM safeguards operations 
  • SAP Cloud ALM enables Clean Core 
  • Transition to SAP Cloud ALM  

How to Register

Registration is quick and easy based on your convenience click on the Register Now link below and secure your spot for one or more sessions. Seats are limited, so don’t wait too long to sign up!

Schedule for February

RegionLanguageDate/TimeRegistration
APJEnglish28 Jan 2025 9:30 AM ISTRecording
EMEA/AmericasEnglish30 Jan 2025 4:00 PM ISTRegister Now

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Choose the time slot that works best for you and your team to attend.

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: About BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG
entry.content:
Thank you for your comment.

The user requirement is to link to CFIN without summarizing BSEG.

However, SAP has responded that implementing BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG is required to avoid errors. (Message No. :FINS_CFIN_AC_DOC 025)

So I would like to know how to implement BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG without summarizing BSEG.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Sales Order is blocked, even although credit limit is...
entry.content: Maintain some future date and retry the process

entry.title: Re: FIORI App "Perform maintenance Job" for work order execution
entry.content: I am facing same issue "no personnel master record exists for person 0" any fixes on this, kindly comment.

entry.title: Re: CAPM - Uncaught TypeError: target.keys is not iterable
entry.content:
add a key to your entity. The easiest would be to use the aspect cuid. 

using {cuid} from '@sap/cds/common';
entity Customers: cuid {
    name    : String(100);
    age     : Integer;
}

entry.title: Re: single PO item multiple AP Invoices
entry.content: You are correct that purchase order will only show most recent AP invoice in Target reference, however, every AP invoice will show the correct PO reference in Base data. So join BaseType, BaseEntry and BaseLine from PCH1 to ObjType, DocEntry and LineNum from POR1.

entry.title: Re: How to generate idoc PODRCH02 using out put type
entry.content: I am also getting the same error. Any answer will be rewarded

entry.title: Re: How to Write a blog
entry.content: well done

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: thanks

entry.title: Re: Calling Email destination from CAPM service using oAu...
entry.content: You may use any option supported by nodemailer. Refer to Send e-mails | SAP Cloud SDK

entry.title: I search a transaction to show all active icf-nodes (abap+web) in a sap system
entry.content: I search a transaction to show all active icf-nodes (abap+web) in a sap system. Developers have activated the services themselves and the system is to be mirrored again. The previously active ICF nodes will then be required again. That's why I would like to get a list of the ICF nodes that have been active so far

entry.title: Re: To trigger workflow in SAP build process Automation f...
entry.content: You can use Workflow API to start a workflow process. API details can be found in api.sap.com.

entry.title: Re: S/4 OnPrem - Is Key User Extensibility "Clean Core" b...
entry.content:
Partially answering myself: Yes, Key User Extensibility has to be kept separated from Developer Extensibility. It's explained here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3ccb50e724b045508fea8b2cf1774b2b.html?locale=en-US#key-user-extensibility-and-developer-extensibility

I still have the doubt on how to organize the Software Components and Packages in a logical way though.

entry.title: Re: Unable to create text id in sales order header and it...
entry.content:
Hi Ashish,

You must be getting this message

Please see as this message is self explanatory.

Regards

Sahil Grover

entry.title: Re: I search a transaction to show all active icf-nodes (abap+web) in a sap system
entry.content: You mean a application that shows you the same info as SICF transaction where you can see disabled ICF nodes greyed out and active ones with regular (dark font) mixed? And you are just looking for a list of "all" active ones without inactive entries?

entry.title: Re: Can't find process and workflows tile in the monitori...
entry.content: These moved into Control Tower/Environments/Public in the latest release.

entry.title: Re: How to generate idoc PODRCH02 using out put type
entry.content:
As far as I know the B129 "No recipient found for message type & in the ALE model" message is triggered in the RECIPIENT_GET_FROM_ALE_MODEL function module. Put a breakpoint there and debug.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Guidance Needed on Exchange Rate Automation for S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
I hope this message finds you well. We are currently evaluating options for automating exchange rate updates in our S/4HANA Cloud environment and would appreciate your guidance on the following:

 

1. For SAP Market Rates Management:

 

• How does the Refinitiv option work in S/4HANA Cloud? Is the integration fully automatic, or are there manual steps involved?

 

• What are the associated costs (e.g., subscription fees)?

 

• Are there any additional setup or configuration requirements?

 

2. For the Bring-Your-Own-Rates (BYOR) option:

 

• What are the prerequisites for connecting a third-party API (e.g., OANDA) to S/4HANA Cloud?

 

• Are there specific tools or configurations needed to build this integration?

 

3. Are there any free or cost-effective APIs you recommend that can provide exchange rate data and integrate with S/4HANA Cloud?

 

Your assistance in understanding these options would be greatly appreciated, as it will help us determine the most efficient approach for our requirements.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Guidance Needed on Exchange Rate Automation for S/4HA...
entry.content:
Please refer the help portal link:- DATAFEED

Configure the currency notation in the App Assign Currency Notations - Datafeed

entry.title: Invoice before doing a good issue in S4
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using S4HANA 2024, in our scope we have an integration with 3rd party warehouse.

My question is , how we can do invoicing before doing a goods issue. 

Is there any standard functionality in S4 which we can use?

As we need to do invoicing after loading in the warehouse , just for the custom clearance.

entry.title: Re: Creating Roles and Managing Authorizations in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
Adding to what Dan said about the lack of support from Build Apps for roles, Note that access control is the responsibility of the backend. Only support from Build Apps is for Authentication. If the user can login, she can access your app. What user have access to is determined by the backend. So look for authorization control in the backend.

SAP Build Apps - Service Guide | Security | SAP Help Portal

Any authenticated users with access to the SAP Build Work Zone tenant will be able to access the Build Apps frontend applications.

Access to all business services and data must be done using BTP Destinations, and the destinations must be configured to use principal propagation. The backend system is responsible for authorization checks when accessing data resources.

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Migrate to Modify or Upload Data in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: What is SAP Migrate?

entry.title: Re: Restrict CR types based on Authorization Group in MDG-BP
entry.content:
Hi @Adi21 ,

You can implement the BADI USMD_CREQUEST_INTEGRATION to filter CR Types. In the method FILTER_CREQUEST_TYPE, you can read the Authorization Group using MDG API and, based on that, filter the CR Types that need to be displayed.

 

Best Regards,

Aditya

entry.title: Re: Copy PO Item Text to GR Item Text
entry.content:
Hello @JJLoverez 

PO item text is a long text, whereas GR item text (MSEG-SGTXT) is a 50 chars field. Therefore I don't think any text determination techniques will work here. 

Your best bet is a custom enhancement with MBCF0002 user exit or MB_MIGO_ITEM_BADI.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: I search a transaction to show all active icf-nodes (abap+web) in a sap system
entry.content: Yes, transaction sicf show all nodes - activ and inactive. But I want to have a list, only with active nodes. This could be a transacion or a report

entry.title: Re: Treasury and Risk Management (TRM / FAM) process and configuration – Example Fixed Term Deposit
entry.content: Very Nice explanation. Thanks for that. I suggest to improve little more explanation of terminology like - AAAR, GVC, PM etc what they are and how these works.
